name: Chicken in Silky Almond Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the chicken dry with a paper towel.
2. Heat a smaller amount of oil in a heavy large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
3. Add the chicken in batches and cook on all sides just until no longer pink (do not brown). Remove using a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. In the same skillet, add remaining oil and sauté sliced onions for about 12 minutes, stirring constantly to ensure even coloring.
5. Stir in almonds, coriander, ginger, cardamom, ground red pepper, cumin, and fennel. Cook for an additional 3 to 5 minutes.
6. Remove the mixture from heat. Transfer half of it to a processor or blender.
7. Puree with half of the yogurt and half of the water. Repeat with the remaining mixture, yogurt, and water.
8. Pour the sauce back into the skillet. Return the chicken to the skillet.
9. Place over medium-high heat and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until the chicken is tender and the sauce thickens, about 45 minutes.
10. Remove from heat. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes (the dish is best refrigerated and reheated). Rewarm gently over low heat.
11. Transfer to a serving dish, garnish with cilantro, and serve immediately.
